concerns.analysis: Several meaningful charts from the source PDF (Figures 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 21, 22, and 23) are missing their image data in the rendered HTML. Instead of valid elements, the output contains literal text placeholders (e.g., '[Image: Line chart showing projections...]') indicating that the image extraction failed (manifest confirms b64_len: 0 for these). Since these are critical data visualizations, their absence constitutes a severe loss of meaningful information.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF contains meaningful data line charts for Figures 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 21, 22, and 23.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML contains literal text placeholders instead of actual image elements, such as: "[Image: Line chart showing projections of Spawning Stock Biomass (SSB) from 2018 to 2050 under different fishing scenarios...]"

concerns.analysis: I examined the list structures throughout the document. Most lists, including complex nested ones (e.g., Page 25 and Page 27), were extracted properly with appropriate child items. However, on Page 14, there is a nested list under the heading 'Amendment 1 Management Carried Forward in Amendment 2'. The second main bullet point ('From Supplement A to the Southern Flounder FMP Amendment 1...') contains three sub-bullets ('A commercial minimum...', 'A minimum mesh...', 'A minimum mesh...'). In the
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
• From Supplement A to the Southern Flounder FMP Amendment 1, as modified by the Aug. 
17, 2017 settlement agreement:
− A commercial minimum size limit of 15 inches TL;
− A minimum mesh size of 6.0-ISM to harvest southern flounder from a gill net; and
− A minimum mesh size of 5.75-ISM stretched mesh for pound net escape panels.

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 2 table(s) with repeated headers across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=2. Examples: pages 47-48: 5 cols (issue, ncdmf, southern flounder advisory committee...); pages 48-49: 5 cols (issue, ncdmf, southern flounder advisory committee...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements with duplicated header rows. The HTML output will show multiple tables instead of one continuous table — a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Output has more images than source PDF (ratio 1.8x) — possible image duplication
concerns.analysis: image_count_ratio=1.77. More images in output than in source indicates duplicated or fabricated image elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 6 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=6, examples=['https://www.implan.com', 'http://www.implan.com/', 'mailto:Michael.loeffler@ncdenr.gov']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "Year"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

The source document uses underlining for emphasis on certain paragraph text (e.g., 'Advisory Committee Recommendations' and 'Southern Flounder FMP Advisory Committee' on page 27). The extraction JSON converts these to markdown italics but drops the actual underline styling. Since markdown does not support underline natively and the pipeline did not preserve it, this represents a loss of visual emphasis.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Advisory Committee Recommendations (Refer to Table 13 for a comparison of recommendations)

Southern Flounder FMP Advisory Committee (Both phrases are underlined in the source PDF)
concerns.output_quote:
*Advisory Committee Recommendations* (Refer to Table 13 for a comparison of recommendations)

*Southern Flounder FMP Advisory Committee*

concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=61. Header/footer elements are present in extraction data. This is common in multi-page government PDFs and is typically cosmetic — the renderer strips them from HTML output.
